name: browser-agent
description: An AI-powered browser agent for VS Code

browserAgent.headless.description: Run the browser in headless mode (no visible window). When false, the browser opens visibly.

browserAgent.targetUrl.description: The URL of the AI website to automate.

browserAgent.promptSelector.description: CSS selector for the text input field on the AI site.

browserAgent.submitSelector.description: CSS selector for the submit button on the AI site.

browserAgent.responseSelector.description: CSS selector for the AI response container.

browserAgent.useSystemChrome.description: Use your installed Google Chrome (with your logged-in sessions) instead of Playwright's bundled Chromium. Requires headless=false.

browserAgent.chromeProfile.description: Chrome profile folder name to use (e.g. 'Default', 'Profile 1', 'Profile 2'). Found inside your Chrome User Data directory.

browserAgent.useCdp.description: Connect to an already-running Chrome via Chrome DevTools Protocol instead of launching a new instance. Chrome must be started with --remote-debugging-port=<cdpPort>. This is the most seamless option — no profile copy needed.

browserAgent.cdpPort.description: The localhost port Chrome is listening on for CDP (used when useCdp=true). Start Chrome with: --remote-debugging-port=9222

browserAgent.persistSession.description: Persist the Playwright browser session between runs (saves cookies/login state in ~/.browser-agent-session). Only applies when useSystemChrome=false.

browserAgent.waitForManualLogin.description: Pause after navigating to the target URL and wait for you to manually log in before the agent types the prompt.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
